allies.rationale_text: Lateral-thinking strengthens brainstorming by turning raw idea volume into genuinely reframed alternatives rather than more of the same answer.
allies.source_quote: Works when idea volume is genuinely valuable because the search space is still underexplored.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Brainstorming conflicts with confirmation-bias when evaluation during generation selectively develops only ideas that confirm the group's existing direction.
structured_tensions.source_quote: brainstorming vs confirmation-bias: brainstorming conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when evaluation of ideas during generation leads participants to selectively develop only the ideas that confirm the group's existing direction rather than genuinely exploring the full possibility space.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Brainstorming conflicts with anchoring when the first idea introduced becomes the reference point that constrains the rest of the session.
structured_tensions.source_quote: brainstorming vs anchoring: brainstorming conflicts with anchoring (anchoring) when the first idea introduced in the session sets an implicit reference point that constrains subsequent idea generation, limiting the diversity of the possibility space.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved one clear divergence ally and the explicit anti-anchoring / anti-confirmation tension block.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader ideation-process sprawl beyond the clearest current-vocabulary seams.
open_questions: Additional divergence allies are source-real, but the current first-order surface is cleanest around lateral-thinking.
